examples.title: Basic User-Defined Exception
examples.description: Demonstrates creating a simple custom exception class by inheriting from std::exception
examples.explanation:
1. We create a class MyException that inherits from std::exception
2. Override the what() method to return a custom error message
3. In main(), we throw MyException() when age is negative
4. The catch block catches the custom exception and prints the message

examples.title: Exception with Custom Data Members
examples.description: Shows how to include additional data members in user-defined exceptions for more detailed error information
examples.explanation:
1. InvalidInputException stores both message and error code
2. Constructor initializes both members
3. getErrorCode() getter allows access to specific error codes
4. The divide() function throws different exceptions based on the error type
5. Catch block demonstrates accessing both what() and getErrorCode()

examples.title: Multiple User-Defined Exceptions in Banking System
examples.description: Advanced example showing practical use of multiple custom exceptions in a realistic banking application
examples.explanation:
1. Base class BankException provides common functionality
2. InsufficientFundsException stores balance and withdrawal amount for detailed error info
3. InvalidAccountException for account validation
4. NegativeAmountException for input validation
5. BankAccount class throws appropriate exceptions for different error conditions
6. Multiple catch blocks handle different exception types specifically
7. This demonstrates practical exception handling in real-world applications
